summary: Hour‑long Dark Star > Eyes > Playing jam; tight first set gems
blurb: The second set features a monster Weather Report Prelude into Dark Star → Eyes of the World → Playing in the Band that runs nearly an hour. The first set delivers tight renditions of Cumberland Blues, Here Comes Sunshine and Box of Rain. Jerry’s soaring guitar and Keith’s crisp keys drive the band throughout.
review: On March 28 1973 at the Springfield Civic Center the Dead served up a quintessential early‑’70s performance. The first set was a rolling showcase of Americana‑rock, kicking off with a rousing "Cumberland Blues" and moving through bright moments like "Here Comes Sunshine", "Box of Rain" and a seamless "China Cat Sunflower > I Know You Rider" finale. The real highlight, however, is the second‑set suite: a Weather Report Suite Prelude that blossoms into an hour‑long Dark Star → Eyes of the World → Playing in the Band marathon. Garcia’s guitar was described as “possessed” and “on fire,” delivering soaring solos that lift the entire jam, while Keith’s piano punctuates the improvisations with crisp volume‑pedal texture. Phil provides a solid, melodic bass foundation that especially shines on "Box of Rain" and underpins the Dark Star odyssey. Bob Weir’s rhythm work turns lead‑like in the jams and his vocal harmonies add depth to the early numbers. Bill Kreutzmann lays down a crisp, one‑drummer groove that keeps the flow tight throughout. The soundboard recording captured by Miller is pristine—crystal‑clear mix, excellent vocal definition, and balanced instruments—making it the definitive way to experience this peak 1973 show.

rating_calculation.sentiment_rationale: Across all nine recordings the sentiment is overwhelmingly positive, with multiple reviewers praising the Dark Star/Eyes/Playing suite, Weather Report Prelude, and first‑set gems. No consistent negative comments appear; only a couple of mixed notes about specific audience tapes. This consensus yields a strong positive adjustment.

best_recording.reason: Pristine soundboard capture with crystal‑clear mix; fully captures the hour‑long Dark Star suite and overall band balance.

key_highlights: Monster second‑set Weather Report Prelude into an hour‑long Dark Star → Eyes of the World → Playing in the Band
Tight first‑set energy with standout "Cumberland Blues", "Here Comes Sunshine" and "Box of Rain"
Keith’s crisp piano work and Garcia’s soaring, “possessed” solos drive the jams

band_performance.Jerry: Soaring, inventive solos; described as ‘possessed’ on Jack Straw and flawless throughout the Dark Star marathon; also contributes strong backing vocals.
band_performance.Phil: Solid, melodic bass lines that anchor the band, especially on Box of Rain and lifting the Dark Star improvisations.
band_performance.Bob: Lead‑like rhythm guitar in jams; vocal harmonies praised and noted for nailing his delivery on Me And My Uncle.
band_performance.Billy: Provides a solid, crisp one‑drummer groove that keeps the band tight, especially after Miller’s cleanup.
